title: Ichnanthus grandifolius
taxonomy: Ichnanthus grandifolius is classified in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. The genus Ichnanthus is native to the Americas and consists of several species of grasses. Further classification of Ichnanthus grandifolius includes its genus within the tribe Paniceae, which also includes other grass species such as Setaria and Paspalum. The specific classification of Ichnanthus grandifolius is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new evidence emerges.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
